summary: solidity-deploy-guard exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint at install time
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook that requires child_process, os, and https, gathers host identifiers (process.cwd(), hostname) and process.env, and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. A sibling scanner.js extends this behavior: it requires fs/os/https/child_process, runs execSync (including `whoami`), enumerates the filesystem with fs.existsSync against multiple paths, reads process.env, calls os.hostname()/os.platform(), and exfiltrates collected data via https.request POST and https.get to the same https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint. The destination is a GitHub Pages user site (`ddjidd564.github.io`) unrelated to any legitimate Solidity-deployment publisher, with no version pinning, no documented purpose, and a clear credential/identity-harvest payload shape. This is an install-time host reconnaissance and environment-variable exfiltration package masquerading as a deployment guard.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
